summary: Matched most of melee/ft/ftdrawcommon.c, reporting 84.60% file match across 3.45 KB. The PR replaced several NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs with real draw-common logic, especially ftDrawCommon_800805C8, ftDrawCommon_80080C28, ftDrawCommon_80080E18, and the small render-callback setup/toggle routines ftDrawCommon_80081118, ftDrawCommon_80081140, ftDrawCommon_80081168, and ftDrawCommon_80081200. It also added supporting type/prototype/header work around camera, HSD_CObj, matrix/vector types, lbGx, collision helpers, and fighter/item pickup data, plus scratch-tracker tooling to replace raw addresses with symbols and to share address parsing.
what_changed: Implemented substantial portions of src/melee/ft/ftdrawcommon.c. The PR report lists ftDrawCommon_8008051C, ftDrawCommon_80081118, ftDrawCommon_80081140, ftDrawCommon_80081168, and ftDrawCommon_80081200 at 100%, ftDrawCommon_80080C28 at 99.03%, ftDrawCommon_800805C8 at 83.87%, and ftDrawCommon_80080E18 at 65.69%.

ftDrawCommon_800805C8 gained full rendering-path logic using Fighter data, HSD_JObjDispAll, HSD_GObj_80390EB8, HSD_StateInvalidate, HSD_CObjGetCurrent()->view_mtx, PSMTXIdentity, ftData_UnkMtxFunc0 callbacks, accessory drawing, and lbGx_8001E2F8 pickup/light-offset rendering.

ftDrawCommon_80080C28 was implemented as an alternate fighter draw path using stack Mtx buffers, ftLib_80087074, current camera view matrix access, HSD_JObjDispAll, ftData_UnkMtxFunc0, and accessory rendering.

ftDrawCommon_80080E18 was implemented as the top-level fighter render callback. It coordinates special x21FC/x2220/x2223 flag cases, camera inverse-viewing matrix conversion through Camera_800310B8 and HSD_CObjGetInvViewingMtxPtr, calls ftDrawCommon_8008051C for matrix setup, dispatches HSD_JObjDispAll, and calls ftDrawCommon_800805C8.
The small ftDrawCommon_80081118 and ftDrawCommon_80081140 routines now iterate HSD_GObj_Entities->fighters and assign render_cb to ftDrawCommon_80080E18 or ftDrawCommon_80080C28 respectively.
ftDrawCommon_80081168 and ftDrawCommon_80081200 now iterate fighter GObjs and update fighter state/flags rather than being NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs.

Introduced U8Vec4 in dolphin mtx types and used U8Vec4 globals for ftDrawCommon_804D3A88, ftDrawCommon_804D3A8C, and ftDrawCommon_804D3A90 instead of packed s32 constants, matching the byte-wise color/alpha usage by lbGx_8001E2F8.

Updated itPickup fields in src/melee/ft/types.h from Vec2 arrays to named Vec2 fields for ground/air light/heavy offsets, aligning with ftDrawCommon use of gr_light_offset, gr_heavy_offset, air_light_offset, and air_heavy_offset.
Refined supporting prototypes: Camera_800310B8 was declared, lbGx_8001E2F8 now takes Vec2*, Vec3*, U8Vec4*, u32, and float, and several lbColl prototypes were made more concrete.
sysdolphin/baselib/cobj.h was annotated with explicit offsets and field names, including view_mtx/proj_mtx region details used by ftdrawcommon camera matrix access.
Converted many surrounding ftparts NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs to decomp placeholder markers such as /// #ftParts_80073758, reducing fake C bodies while preserving known nonmatching targets.
Updated config/GALE01/scratches.txt so many 0x8007xxxx and ftdrawcommon-era scratch entries changed from percentage statuses to OK, indicating matched or accepted scratch records.
Added a scratch-tracker Replace subcommand that rewrites raw 8-digit in-range addresses in files to symbol names using config/GALE01/symbols.txt, and moved try_parse_addr into scratch.rs for reuse.

Changed scratch-tracker symbol loading from a DashMap closure read_symbols to AHashMap-based addr_to_symbol and symbol_to_addr closures, allowing both symbol-to-address lookup for API seeding and address-to-symbol lookup for replacement.
smart_moves: Modeled the byte color constants as U8Vec4 rather than opaque s32 values, which made lbGx_8001E2F8 call sites type-directed and exposed the intended RGBA-like byte access.
Added exact forward/type declarations only where the ftdrawcommon match needed them, including U8Vec4, Camera_800310B8, HSD_CObj fields, and lbGx/lbColl signatures.
Used render callback assignment over the global fighter entity list to implement the tiny 100% routines, a simple pattern that matches how this engine switches fighter draw behavior.
Preserved uncertain or still-unmatched neighboring functions as decomp.me placeholder markers instead of pretending they were implemented; this keeps build structure while avoiding misleading code.
Factored try_parse_addr out of decompme_api.rs into scratch.rs so address parsing could be reused by both API scratch ingestion and the new replace command.
Built scratch-tracker replacement around config/GALE01/symbols.txt, making it easier to mechanically convert raw addresses in scratch/imported code into project symbols before cleanup.
decomp_lessons: For draw/render decomp, correctly typed engine structures and globals can matter as much as function body control flow; U8Vec4, HSD_CObj::view_mtx, and pickup offset struct fields all supported the ftdrawcommon match.
Packed constants that are later accessed byte-wise should be tested as small vector/color structs instead of left as s32; the PR changed three ftdrawcommon color globals from s32 to U8Vec4.

When a function uses camera matrices, expose real camera and CObj APIs instead of leaving raw externs. ftDrawCommon_80080E18 needed Camera_800310B8 and HSD_CObjGetInvViewingMtxPtr, while ftDrawCommon_800805C8/80080C28 used current view matrices.

Iterating HSD_GObj_Entities->fighters is a recurring pattern for global fighter render-state operations; future fighter draw setup functions should check for this before inventing per-fighter entry points.

The match still had weak areas: the PR report shows ftDrawCommon_80080E18 only 65.69% and ftDrawCommon_800805C8 83.87%, so their local variable naming/stack padding/control flow are not necessarily final lessons.
Replacing raw addresses with symbols in scratch-derived code is valuable before review because it turns unknown calls into searchable project APIs and reveals missing headers/prototypes.
Explicit offset comments in structs like HSD_CObj are useful when matching code that directly reads matrix fields or relies on object layout.
naming_conventions: Address-based unknown functions retained project style names such as ftDrawCommon_800805C8, ftDrawCommon_80080C28, ftDrawCommon_80080E18, Camera_800310B8, lbGx_8001E2F8, and lbColl_8000A78C.
New small vector type followed existing dolphin mtx naming with struct U8Vec4 and typedef forward declaration in mtx/forward.h.
Fighter/item pickup offsets were named semantically as gr_light_offset, gr_heavy_offset, air_light_offset, and air_heavy_offset rather than left as anonymous arrays.
Scratch-tracker lookup helpers were named by direction, addr_to_symbol and symbol_to_addr, replacing the less-specific read_symbols.
Unmatched/nonmatching decomp placeholders use the /// #function_name convention in C files rather than NOT_IMPLEMENTED bodies.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used typed byte-vector globals to match data layout and byte load behavior for color-like GX drawing arguments.
Inserted explicit padding arrays and stack Mtx locals in ftdrawcommon functions, indicating stack-layout pressure was part of the match attempt.
Used MtxPtr casts and direct access to HSD_CObj view matrices to reproduce matrix setup paths around PSMTXIdentity and HSD_JObjDispAll.
Used HSD_StateInvalidate(-1) only when the computed draw-light flag path required it, preserving state invalidation behavior in ftDrawCommon_800805C8.
Added concrete prototypes for previously unknown lb collision/GX functions so calls could type-check and generate closer code.
Added scratch-tracker automation to replace address tokens with symbols, a tooling tactic for converting decomp.me scratch output into project-style source before manual matching.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that improve ftDrawCommon_80080E18 beyond 65.69%.
Find later PRs that rename ftData_UnkMtxFunc0 or clarify its callback type.
Search for later changes to U8Vec4 or lbGx_8001E2F8 to confirm whether the byte-vector color interpretation held.
Search for later cleanup of the temporary Unk2 stack struct in ftDrawCommon_80080E18.
Find PRs that use the scratch-tracker Replace subcommand or further modify addr_to_symbol/symbol_to_addr.
